post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 240
Newbie Game 94




To the left, down the hall, up the narrow staircase, and atop the tower is one of those great and formidable beings whom all call Granny and none call old, whose words all revere and none follow, whose sanity all fear and none wholly trust. Indeed, man scarcely bows before the more ancient, the wiser, the saner, the grander; instead, he likes to forget it. If such a being's grandeur surpasses the limits of human denial, then man, aghast by the dazzle, stuffs it away in a hidden chamber somewhere. Such was the unsung fate all the most prestigious of Great Granny's kind bore. The lesser, the men forgot; the greater, the men concealed from their progeny. History, then, thus lies sorely bare of the majestic feats of Grannies, disguising the truth with the milder names of Charles, Caesar, and Euclid. Yet the Grannies never mutinied at the negligence. From the stone attic where she was stowed, the last of the race, Great Granny, instructs imperiously each and every budding Unpaid Intern. From this spacious room and its hearty fire sprang the first of the Unpaid Interns, from the maxims of the collective annals of her line developed the interns' sense of duty, and from under her sagacious watch they were destined to find their Boss.


- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules.

For experienced players: this is the only tab you need to read.
For new players: Please familiarize yourself with the rest of the rules as well.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "day" is 2 weeks while each "night" is 3 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the Matrix6 setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by HannahW and Scipio1.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (a_tps_person#0298/scipio#4398).
  • For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go here. State the merriam-webster definition of a word in the opening post flavor as evidence that you have read this post.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 72 hours (3 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, please click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, please click here.

  • Moderation Policies
    This tab codifies several moderation procedures.

    • Role Randomization

    When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. Both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are thus possible.

    Current RNG Process (Matrix6)
    Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized by the Random Integer Generator:



    The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab.

    Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized using the List Randomizer and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank and then by alphabetical order. The ordered playerlist of each game is in the Alive/Dead tabs.


    • Tiebreaking

    If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting.

    • Votes cast during deadline

    If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner.

    Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count.

    • Mafia Night Kill Disagreement

    The moderators encourage the Mafia members to discuss the night kill using the private chat and agree upon a kill. If the players cannot arrive at a conclusion by the night deadline and continue to be in disagreement, however, the mafia factional kill will not go through. The moderators will not arbitrate this disagreement.

    • Game-advancing Posts

    A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks.

    • Postbans

    Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length.

    Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help.

    You may contact the moderators through discord at a_tps_person#0289 or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS.

    • Outside Consulting

    If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.

    • Out Of Thread

    Due to clarifications by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as flavor, gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.

  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • HannahW and Scipio1 are the moderators of Mafia Game 240.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG240 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) version 8.
    Current version: 8.7
    Version History
Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 4/A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 5/B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 6/C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Mafia have daychat.
Role PMs
Apprentice Intern (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are an Apprentice Intern (Vanilla Townie).

Somehow or another you or your guardians caught wind of a new organization that was apparently taking interns or something. And in yet another way you heard the rumor that it was destined for glory, so here you are, marching off each day with the dawn to an archaic-looking tower on the fringe of an insignificant city. On your first day, you were immediately disillusioned concerning the great glory that drove you here. Great Granny's very first sentence directly declared that. On the second day, you were taught to memorize the Unpaid Interns' purpose statement. On the third day, you discovered that you were training to become a full-fledged Unpaid Intern like Great Granny's assistant… and so you continued to be educated.

That is, until now. There you were, sitting obediently at your desk, pondering duty when Great Granny literally reeled over and died. Apparently, someone had decided that she was too great, didn't like that, and sent two assassins to poison her breakfast. Now you must execute justice and carry on her legacy. That begins with identifying the killers, which you start immediately with your best sense of duty, which might have been encouraged by the fact that the staircase has mysteriously disappeared and you're trapped up here.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Diligent Apprentice Intern (Town Doctor) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are the Diligent Apprentice Intern (Town Doctor).

When you first climbed the rickety stairs to the attic of the tower, you were dismayed. The furnishings of your classroom seemed archaic compared to the overpriced furniture you had at home, and the existence of a fireplace seemed like a hazard. It didn't seem like a place of great advancement and progress as you'd hoped. However, when you finally turned your attention to your ancient teacher's words, you were astounded. You nodded at every word and took to heart all she taught. You decided that perhaps progress isn't mainly technological advancements but improvements in the nature of humanity.

After the first few days, Great Granny noticed your greater diligence and gave you an ancient book on healing from her shelf for you to take home and study. Since then, you've read what you could by flashlight under the covers until you fell asleep every night. Although your knowledge is still rudimentary and your hand untried, you can recollect enough to heal a person every night with the remnants of Granny's medicine cabinet, but it can take you hours. Nevertheless, you will do your best to keep your fellow interns alive, even if you must go without sleep. Great Granny's legacy is at risk.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to heal and to prevent from dying that night. You can save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Inquisitive Apprentice Intern (Town Tracker) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are the Inquisitive Apprentice Intern (Town Tracker).

You first discovered the existence of this Unpaid Intern business by following someone. In your defense, you had gotten lost in the countryside, and your uncomfortable substitute bed had prodded you awake at unearthly hours. When you saw some kids just randomly trotting down the road when you could barely even see, you were curious and decided to follow despite almost tripping on every other stone. Thus you fell victim to Great Granny's inescapable sight. As you were turning away from the unimpressive stone tower, Great Granny had sent her assistant to fetch you, for she had seen you from afar and noticed your absence. Then and there, you were enrolled.

Great Granny had then commented that your inquisitiveness and inability to sleep on harder surfaces might come in handy sometime. You had thought it outrageous then, but now it seems prophetic. A stone floor, even covered with a nice rug, only gives you an hour of sleep at most, so you instead have decided to pass the time watching one or another of the other apprentices to see if they stir at night. However, as before, you're really bad at seeing in the dark, so you can't tell what exactly they do. At least you do your best, you tell yourself. Great Granny would have been passably pleased with that.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to track. You will then be informed of who that player visited or targeted, if any, that night. You may also receive "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Good-for-Something Apprentice Intern (Town Jailkeeper) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are the Good-for-Something Apprentice Intern (Town Jailkeeper).

Your father is an acclaimed architect. Your mother is an accomplished artist. Every one of your siblings is an expert or promising student in law or science or politics or art. Then there's you. You had no aspirations, and you simply wanted to enjoy your childhood, but your father would not permit that. Academy after academy and tutor after tutor finally landed you at his final resort: some obscure Unpaid Intern program run by some obscure ancient granny. That at least, your father had said, might teach you to be good for something.

The first thing you had noticed when you arrived at the foot of the stone tower was that it was the exact one made from one of the plans your father had given to you as scratch paper when he was attempting to get you into math. It had been one of his earliest projects and was nothing of note, but you did recall a secret chamber somewhere in the attic which, incidentally, was exactly where Great Granny was headquartered. After a few furtive searches, you found the spot ― just large enough to fit a single person ― although nothing had come of it till now. Now, you have resolved to use it to hide a person each night: you will drug them then lug them into the hidden room. It's the least you can do to fulfill the last wishes of Great Granny, and it's the best you can do to perform your duty as an apprentice Unpaid Intern.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to lock up for the night. That player will be protected from all night actions and cannot perform any night actions. You cannot target yourself.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Thoughtful Apprentice Intern (Town Cop) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are the Thoughtful Apprentice Intern (Town Cop).

You were perhaps one of the few actively invited to become an Unpaid Intern. Somehow, Great Granny must have noticed you wandering the countryside with those piercing eyes of her from her little tower. Since you had little else to do and no harm could come of it, you assented. You have been quite intrigued by the lessons so far, and Great Granny has been more than willing to hold extensive philosophical conversations with you. Thus, you have been content.

Upon her murder, however, you lost your mentor. In an effort to avenge her, you were forced to turn from philosophy to psychological analysis of each of the apprentices to figure out their true alignments. You are fairly unacquainted with the field, but as Great Granny said, you have a great mind. So, although it will take you all night, you will sit in the silence and think and remember and think and analyze and think, exhausting all the methods and theories you can think of, and then think again until you are confident in your results.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to investigate. You will then be informed of their alignment. You may also receive "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Inattentive Apprentice Intern(1-shot Bulletproof Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are the Inattentive Apprentice Intern (1-shot Bulletproof Townie).

You are the most troublesome and energetic of all of Great Granny's current students. That doesn't mean you're disobedient ― you simply take a good five minutes to somewhat settle down into following whatever she might instruct you to do: five minutes to sit down, five minutes to open the book to the right page, five minutes to repeat the Unpaid Interns' purpose statement… The one thing you do on time is arrive on time because there is a line Great Granny will never permit anyone to budge, so you conceded.

The dawn of the day Great Granny died, you were trotting along unawares, thinking only of your birthday the day before. Your parents had gotten you a new laptop, your grandparents had given you heaps of money, and your cousin had given you a bulletproof vest, which, you thought, was great and more interesting than the rest. As you tramped through the wooded roads, you thought of how, if anyone shot at you, you would survive. As you creaked up the narrow tower stairs, you thought of how, if anyone were to ambush you, you would live. As you sat in the attic, you thought of how, if anyone were to fly by in a hot air balloon and level a gun at you, you would not die. And now, if any of the nasty fake interns were to try to kill you, you will likewise not die ― you hope.

The first time the Mafia shoot you, you will survive, but after that, if you are shot again, you will be killed. Your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Fake Apprentice Intern (Mafia Goon) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are a Fake Apprentice Intern (Mafia Goon).

One day a stranger randomly walked up to you and accosted you, promising great reward if you would give him a few minutes to state his case. You'd heard that age old adage "don't talk to strangers," but some nice reward sounded nice to you at the moment (your parents had recently taken your allowance away) so you agreed. All he told you that day was to enroll as a student in some random school where an ancient granny taught, and he would give you some money to start with. Your parents gladly signed you up when you suggested it, for it seemed to them that you were starting to show interest in some kind of work. There, however, was where the trouble started.

In order to get your daily allowance, which was much greater than what your parents ever ventured to give you, from that stranger, you had to give him a daily report of the teachings of the ancient granny. It seemed innocent then, and, though not particularly interesting, you thought the granny's lessons weren't too bad, though abominably painful to actually put into practice. Then, one day, the stranger introduced you to your fellow student, [player], who was likewise in on the scheme. Your job, he told you, was to poison the breakfast you gave Granny one day. Your partner would do so too, just in case. Because you wanted and needed the money, because the stranger was really intimidating, because the other student didn't show signs of backing out, because one ancient granny off the earth wouldn't do anything, because no one would even care if the ancient granny disappeared… because of all this, you tell yourself, you assented. But you had not anticipated being trapped in the tower with the other apprentices who would certainly find you out soon, if you don't do something.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player.
You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Counterfeit Apprentice Intern (Mafia Roleblocker) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are a Counterfeit Apprentice Intern (Mafia Roleblocker).

Unlike your partner, [player], you were not randomly picked off the street. Mr. Stranger found you at the agreed place one day and told you he had another job for which he needed to hire you, a child criminal. It would require dressing up more nicely and attending school, he said. It didn't sound too hard, and it was just ridding the earth of one ancient granny who would die anyway, in the words of Mr. Stranger. At any rate, the pay was remarkably high, and he told you that you would get to be called the "counterfeit" apprentice intern, not just the "fake" one, unlike the other kid. You didn't know what that meant, but the way he said it, you assume that it entails greater authority and expertise.

Indeed, you came prepared with some doses of a sleeping drug just in case things didn't go as planned ― and they didn't. For some reason, the stairs decided to fall apart, trapping all of you up in the tower. You're pretty sure that, if left there's no one to bother you, you could find a way out, but all the apprentice interns seem driven but Great Granny's last words about something random about duty to find the killers, so you can't say anything without getting suspected. Instead, you'll use your sleeping potions to prevent them from doing anything sneaky at night. That, paired with your master deception skills will surely get you out of this mess which is completely that ancient granny's fault…

You may discuss with your partner, [player] ([role]), in this QuickTopic while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You can simultaneously perform both the factional kill and your roleblock.
You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[center][size=175]Welcome to Mafia Game 240[/size]
[size=125]Newbie Game 94

[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/867949223113543700/image0.jpg[/img]


[size=100]To the left, down the hall, up the narrow staircase, and atop the tower is one of those great and formidable beings whom all call Granny and none call old, whose words all revere and none follow, whose sanity all fear and none wholly trust. Indeed, man scarcely bows before the more ancient, the wiser, the saner, the grander; instead, he likes to forget it. If such a being's grandeur surpasses the limits of human denial, then man, aghast by the dazzle, stuffs it away in a hidden chamber somewhere. Such was the unsung fate all the most prestigious of Great Granny's kind bore. The lesser, the men forgot; the greater, the men concealed from their progeny. History, then, thus lies sorely bare of the majestic feats of Grannies, disguising the truth with the milder names of Charles, Caesar, and Euclid. Yet the Grannies never mutinied at the negligence. From the stone attic where she was stowed, the last of the race, Great Granny, instructs imperiously each and every budding Unpaid Intern. From this spacious room and its hearty fire sprang the first of the Unpaid Interns, from the maxims of the collective annals of her line developed the interns' sense of duty, and from under her sagacious watch they were destined to find their Boss.[/center]


[hide=- Important Rules -]
[b]This tab summarizes the most important rules.[/b]

[b]For experienced players:[/b] this is the only tab you need to read. 
[b]For new players:[/b] Please familiarize yourself with the rest of the rules as well. 

[b]Integrity[/b]
[list][*][b]Do not edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*][b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request. 
[*][b]Do not gamethrow[/b] or intentionally sabotage your wincondition. 
[*][b]Do not attach files[/b] or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
[*][b]Do not use OGI[/b] (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.[/list]

[b]Activity[/b]
[list][*]Each [b]"day" is 2 weeks[/b] while each [b]"night" is 3 days[/b].
[*][b]Do not post during night.[/b]
[*]This game uses [b]Dawn start;[/b] Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules. 
[*]All [b]times[/b] will be in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC-5) or [b]Eastern Daylight Time[/b] (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable. 
[*]Activity rules are [b]48 hours to in-game prod[/b] and [b]72 hours to PM prod.[/b][/list]

[b]Voting[/b]
[list][*]You [b]must format your votes correctly.[/b] Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated. 
[*][b]Majority hang is off[/b] for Day 1, but it is [b]on[/b] starting Day 2.
[*][b]Deadline hang is on[/b] from Day 1 but it is [b]off[/b] for LYLO. 
[*]Votes are [b]locked at LYLO.[/b][/list]

[b]Etiquette and Warnings[/b]

[list][*][b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*]Do not use formatting that makes text [b]hard to read.[/b]
[*]You may attack a player’s opinion, but [b]do not make personal attacks[/b] on players themselves. 
[*]First-timers can receive up to [b]3 warnings[/b] before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. [b]All other players can receive up to 2. [/b]
[*][b]All other rules are standard.[/b] If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.[/list] 

[b]Setup and Moderators[/b]

[list][*]This game is based on the [b]Matrix6 setup. [/b]
[*]Mafia have access to [b]daychat[/b]. 
[*]All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are [b]standard. [/b]
[*]This game is moderated by HannahW and Scipio1. 
[*]This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.[/list]

[b]Resources and Support[/b]

[list][*]For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (a_tps_person#0298/scipio#4398). 
[*]For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below. 
[*]For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c1980342h2517106_important_resources]here[/url]. State the merriam-webster definition of a word in the opening post flavor as evidence that you have read this post.
[*]If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.[/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=http://bit.ly/fbith]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmchao]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmww123]willwang123[/url], and [url=http://bit.ly/ffdas]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=http://bit.ly/MUnFR]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*]Do not post during the night phase.
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON. 
[*]If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered. 
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Votes must be in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b] to be counted.
[*]Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames. 
[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 72 hours (3 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative. [/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335214]please click here.[/url]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335246]please click here.[/url][/hide]

[*][hide=Moderation Policies]
[b]This tab codifies several moderation procedures.[/b]


[list][*]Role Randomization[/list]

When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. Both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are thus possible. 

[hide=Current RNG Process (Matrix6)]
Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized by the [url=https://www.random.org/integers/]Random Integer Generator[/url]: 

[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/708770843731165224/762748231598997534/unknown.png[/img]

The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab. 

Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized using the [url=https://www.random.org/lists/]List Randomizer[/url] and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank and then by alphabetical order. The ordered playerlist of each game is in the Alive/Dead tabs.[/hide]


[list][*]Tiebreaking[/list]

If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting. 


[list][*]Votes cast during deadline[/list]

If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner. 

Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count. 


[list][*]Mafia Night Kill Disagreement[/list]

The moderators encourage the Mafia members to discuss the night kill using the private chat and agree upon a kill. If the players cannot arrive at a conclusion by the night deadline and continue to be in disagreement, however, the mafia factional kill will not go through. The moderators will not arbitrate this disagreement. 


[list][*]Game-advancing Posts[/list]

A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks. 


[list][*]Postbans[/list]

Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length. 

Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help. 

You may contact the moderators through discord at [b]a_tps_person#0289[/b] or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS. 


[list][*]Outside Consulting[/list]

If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.


[list][*]Out Of Thread[/list]

Due to [url=http://bit.ly/fmooc]clarifications[/url] by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as flavor, gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]HannahW and Scipio1 are the moderators of Mafia Game 240. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green ([color=#00f752]#00f752[/color]), Activity Check blue ([color=#005dff]#005dff[/color]), HannahW's blue ([color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color]), Scipio1's blue ([color=#3d70ff]#3d70ff[/color]).
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game. [/list]

[b]MG240 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) version 8.[/b]
[i]Current version: 8.7[/i]
[url=https://aops.com/community/p21335350]Version History[/url]
[/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=http://bit.ly/m6msw]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*][b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 4/A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 5/B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 6/C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*]This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*]Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
[*]Cop results are sane.
[*]Mafia have daychat.[/list]
[hide=Role PMs]
[quote="Apprentice Intern (Vanilla Townie)"]
Hello, [player]! You are an [color=#00f752]Apprentice Intern (Vanilla Townie)[/color].

Somehow or another you or your guardians caught wind of a new organization that was apparently taking interns or something. And in yet another way you heard the rumor that it was destined for glory, so here you are, marching off each day with the dawn to an archaic-looking tower on the fringe of an insignificant city. On your first day, you were immediately disillusioned concerning the great glory that drove you here. Great Granny's very first sentence directly declared that. On the second day, you were taught to memorize the Unpaid Interns' purpose statement. On the third day, you discovered that you were training to become a full-fledged Unpaid Intern like Great Granny's assistant… and so you continued to be educated.

That is, until now. There you were, sitting obediently at your desk, pondering duty when Great Granny literally reeled over and died. Apparently, someone had decided that she was too great, didn't like that, and sent two assassins to poison her breakfast. Now you must execute justice and carry on her legacy. That begins with identifying the killers, which you start immediately with your best sense of duty, which might have been encouraged by the fact that the staircase has mysteriously disappeared and you're trapped up here.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Diligent Apprentice Intern (Town Doctor)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Diligent Apprentice Intern (Town Doctor)[/color].

When you first climbed the rickety stairs to the attic of the tower, you were dismayed. The furnishings of your classroom seemed archaic compared to the overpriced furniture you had at home, and the existence of a fireplace seemed like a hazard. It didn't seem like a place of great advancement and progress as you'd hoped. However, when you finally turned your attention to your ancient teacher's words, you were astounded. You nodded at every word and took to heart all she taught. You decided that perhaps progress isn't mainly technological advancements but improvements in the nature of humanity.

After the first few days, Great Granny noticed your greater diligence and gave you an ancient book on healing from her shelf for you to take home and study. Since then, you've read what you could by flashlight under the covers until you fell asleep every night. Although your knowledge is still rudimentary and your hand untried, you can recollect enough to heal a person every night with the remnants of Granny's medicine cabinet, but it can take you hours. Nevertheless, you will do your best to keep your fellow interns alive, even if you must go without sleep. Great Granny's legacy is at risk.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to heal and to prevent from dying that night. You can save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Inquisitive Apprentice Intern (Town Tracker)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Inquisitive Apprentice Intern (Town Tracker)[/color].

You first discovered the existence of this Unpaid Intern business by following someone. In your defense, you had gotten lost in the countryside, and your uncomfortable substitute bed had prodded you awake at unearthly hours. When you saw some kids just randomly trotting down the road when you could barely even see, you were curious and decided to follow despite almost tripping on every other stone. Thus you fell victim to Great Granny's inescapable sight. As you were turning away from the unimpressive stone tower, Great Granny had sent her assistant to fetch you, for she had seen you from afar and noticed your absence. Then and there, you were enrolled.

Great Granny had then commented that your inquisitiveness and inability to sleep on harder surfaces might come in handy sometime. You had thought it outrageous then, but now it seems prophetic. A stone floor, even covered with a nice rug, only gives you an hour of sleep at most, so you instead have decided to pass the time watching one or another of the other apprentices to see if they stir at night. However, as before, you're really bad at seeing in the dark, so you can't tell what exactly they do. At least you do your best, you tell yourself. Great Granny would have been passably pleased with that.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to track. You will then be informed of who that player visited or targeted, if any, that night. You may also receive "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Good-for-Something Apprentice Intern (Town Jailkeeper)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Good-for-Something Apprentice Intern (Town Jailkeeper)[/color].

Your father is an acclaimed architect. Your mother is an accomplished artist. Every one of your siblings is an expert or promising student in law or science or politics or art. Then there's you. You had no aspirations, and you simply wanted to enjoy your childhood, but your father would not permit that. Academy after academy and tutor after tutor finally landed you at his final resort: some obscure Unpaid Intern program run by some obscure ancient granny. That at least, your father had said, might teach you to be good for something.

The first thing you had noticed when you arrived at the foot of the stone tower was that it was the exact one made from one of the plans your father had given to you as scratch paper when he was attempting to get you into math. It had been one of his earliest projects and was nothing of note, but you did recall a secret chamber somewhere in the attic which, incidentally, was exactly where Great Granny was headquartered. After a few furtive searches, you found the spot ― just large enough to fit a single person ― although nothing had come of it till now. Now, you have resolved to use it to hide a person each night: you will drug them then lug them into the hidden room. It's the least you can do to fulfill the last wishes of Great Granny, and it's the best you can do to perform your duty as an apprentice Unpaid Intern.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to lock up for the night. That player will be protected from all night actions and cannot perform any night actions. You cannot target yourself.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Thoughtful Apprentice Intern (Town Cop)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Thoughtful Apprentice Intern (Town Cop)[/color].

You were perhaps one of the few actively invited to become an Unpaid Intern. Somehow, Great Granny must have noticed you wandering the countryside with those piercing eyes of her from her little tower. Since you had little else to do and no harm could come of it, you assented. You have been quite intrigued by the lessons so far, and Great Granny has been more than willing to hold extensive philosophical conversations with you. Thus, you have been content.

Upon her murder, however, you lost your mentor. In an effort to avenge her, you were forced to turn from philosophy to psychological analysis of each of the apprentices to figure out their true alignments. You are fairly unacquainted with the field, but as Great Granny said, you have a great mind. So, although it will take you all night, you will sit in the silence and think and remember and think and analyze and think, exhausting all the methods and theories you can think of, and then think again until you are confident in your results.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player to investigate. You will then be informed of their alignment. You may also receive "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Inattentive Apprentice Intern(1-shot Bulletproof Townie)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Inattentive Apprentice Intern (1-shot Bulletproof Townie)[/color].

You are the most troublesome and energetic of all of Great Granny's current students. That doesn't mean you're disobedient ― you simply take a good five minutes to somewhat settle down into following whatever she might instruct you to do: five minutes to sit down, five minutes to open the book to the right page, five minutes to repeat the Unpaid Interns' purpose statement… The one thing you do on time is arrive on time because there is a line Great Granny will never permit anyone to budge, so you conceded.

The dawn of the day Great Granny died, you were trotting along unawares, thinking only of your birthday the day before. Your parents had gotten you a new laptop, your grandparents had given you heaps of money, and your cousin had given you a bulletproof vest, which, you thought, was great and more interesting than the rest. As you tramped through the wooded roads, you thought of how, if anyone shot at you, you would survive. As you creaked up the narrow tower stairs, you thought of how, if anyone were to ambush you, you would live. As you sat in the attic, you thought of how, if anyone were to fly by in a hot air balloon and level a gun at you, you would not die. And now, if any of the nasty fake interns were to try to kill you, you will likewise not die ― you hope.

The first time the Mafia shoot you, you will survive, but after that, if you are shot again, you will be killed. Your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest.
You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Fake Apprentice Intern (Mafia Goon)"]
Hello, [player]! You are a [color=red]Fake Apprentice Intern (Mafia Goon)[/color].

One day a stranger randomly walked up to you and accosted you, promising great reward if you would give him a few minutes to state his case. You'd heard that age old adage "don't talk to strangers," but some nice reward sounded nice to you at the moment (your parents had recently taken your allowance away) so you agreed. All he told you that day was to enroll as a student in some random school where an ancient granny taught, and he would give you some money to start with. Your parents gladly signed you up when you suggested it, for it seemed to them that you were starting to show interest in some kind of work. There, however, was where the trouble started.

In order to get your daily allowance, which was much greater than what your parents ever ventured to give you, from that stranger, you had to give him a daily report of the teachings of the ancient granny. It seemed innocent then, and, though not particularly interesting, you thought the granny's lessons weren't too bad, though abominably painful to actually put into practice. Then, one day, the stranger introduced you to your fellow student, [player], who was likewise in on the scheme. Your job, he told you, was to poison the breakfast you gave Granny one day. Your partner would do so too, just in case. Because you wanted and needed the money, because the stranger was really intimidating, because the other student didn't show signs of backing out, because one ancient granny off the earth wouldn't do anything, because no one would even care if the ancient granny disappeared… because of all this, you tell yourself, you assented. But you had not anticipated being trapped in the tower with the other apprentices who would certainly find you out soon, if you don't do something.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player.
You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Counterfeit Apprentice Intern (Mafia Roleblocker)"]
Hello, [player]! You are a [color=red]Counterfeit Apprentice Intern (Mafia Roleblocker)[/color].

Unlike your partner, [player], you were not randomly picked off the street. Mr. Stranger found you at the agreed place one day and told you he had another job for which he needed to hire you, a child criminal. It would require dressing up more nicely and attending school, he said. It didn't sound too hard, and it was just ridding the earth of one ancient granny who would die anyway, in the words of Mr. Stranger. At any rate, the pay was remarkably high, and he told you that you would get to be called the "counterfeit" apprentice intern, not just the "fake" one, unlike the other kid. You didn't know what that meant, but the way he said it, you assume that it entails greater authority and expertise.

Indeed, you came prepared with some doses of a sleeping drug just in case things didn't go as planned ― and they didn't. For some reason, the stairs decided to fall apart, trapping all of you up in the tower. You're pretty sure that, if left there's no one to bother you, you could find a way out, but all the apprentice interns seem driven but Great Granny's last words about something random about duty to find the killers, so you can't say anything without getting suspected. Instead, you'll use your sleeping potions to prevent them from doing anything sneaky at night. That, paired with your master deception skills will surely get you out of this mess which is completely that ancient granny's fault…

You may discuss with your partner, [player] ([role]), in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You can simultaneously perform both the factional kill and your roleblock.
You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote][/hide]

post_rendered:
Most people 120 years old aren't even alive anymore. Great Granny is. Although besieged by a profusion of wrinkles, her eyes can still identify a kid from miles off.
"They're all coming on in a bunch," she remarks from the attic's sole window. I take this as a cue to finish tidying up the already tidy table and join her.

Fortunately, the window overlooks not the sultry city, but the countryside, albeit a clogged one. Unnecessary roads, lawns, and mansions chop it up into bits of forest here, slivers of stream there, and snippets of field beyond. Already, the grasses and treetops have caught the dawn, and all the running water for miles around is busy rushing from one sparkling spot of sun to the next, but the man-made roads remain sunken in pinkish grey shadow. Other than a small cluster of dark figures coming down a road in the distance, humanity lies silent. Only the deer and water and squirrels and bees have begun their day. And us.

"They'll arrive right on time," Great Granny smiles as a breeze sweeps her greying hair into her face.
I nod. The first thing she always teaches the aspiring Unpaid Interns is to be prompt, on time. Often, that means rising while the sun has just begun to color the deep night sky and trotting over, breakfast in hand. Here in the tower, we ― Great Granny and I ― do likewise, although we cannot stroll out. Instead, I straighten up everything again and then join Great Granny at perusing an ancient volume from her meager library of the Granny Annals. The collection used to be massive, but now, along with its keepers, it has been reduced to a single shelf with a lone caretaker: the last of the Grannies.

Great Granny has gravely confided to me that she hopes that, as her last legacy, she can raise a new race to take the place of the Grannies. "Unpaid Interns," she reminds me sometimes, "will accomplish great feats out of duty and honor alone. Although unrecognized, they will accomplish their work and more with extreme excellence. Unpaid Interns will carry mankind onward and upward along the path the Grannies have forged once I am gone." I always murmur in assent when she says this, as she does again now, gazing upon the rising sun.
"This dawning sun," she continues, waving her workworn hand at the golden star, "comes every day in unseen glory. Do the lords or workers of humanity wake to watch its beauty? No, they lie slothfully in bed. I do not condemn them for succumbing to their common nature, but our purposes are greater than theirs: Grannies have for ages done their duty and a little more, bearing people at large up the difficult path of progress. After I die, Unpaid Interns will continue our legacy with this sun."

I nod yet again. When I had first become Great Granny's assistant, I had scoffed cynically to myself at the self-importance and unrealistic grandness of her scheme. Since then, however, I have recognized the understatement. She can say far fancier and loftier things while remaining wholly realistic. My cynicism and that of others, I realized, is only founded on our own experience with mankind ― the portion and history without Grannies. Thus, I always nod sincerely now, though for the moment I doubt that Great Granny will ever die: at 120, she remains far more fit and healthy than even me, and her energy reserve surpasses even that of the nine incoming children combined.

Sometimes I wonder why she even has an assistant. Other times I wonder why I even am her assistant.


The earlier one begins at school, the sooner one learns its lessons… ~ GA Henty says Great Granny, explaining one reason why she requires her students to rise promptly with the sun.
Before the game can begin, you must read all the rules (especially the important ones) and confirm that you have done this (the rules will tell you how). Please listen to Great Granny's advice and do so immediately that the game can begin quickly and your mafia education can commence posthaste.

You may now post.

post_rendered:
Hello everyone,

It occurred to me that this never actually gets explained, so let me give you the really, really basic rundown of how this game works.

Everyone here is either town, who wants to kill the mafia by voting them out, or mafia, who want to live long enough to kill the entire town. Townies don’t want to get voted out, because that’s more time that the mafia isn’t getting voted out, and the mafia doesn’t want to get voted out for obvious reasons. Town has the majority of votes, so the voting will be, for the most part, meant to kill the mafia.

This means that everyone wants others to think that they are town.

This means you shouldn’t take it too seriously when someone says “I’m town” or “I’m scum” (scum meaning mafia). If they say “I’m town”, well duh, anyone would say that, and if they say “I’m scum”, they’re clearly joking.

Now, for voting people out, or hanging.

Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player killed by the mafia. Night kills are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.

Lastly, what will we actually do for most of this game? The bread and butter of Mafia is the day phases, where we just... talk. The core idea is that you can often figure out if players are town or mafia just by reading their posts. This is also known as scumhunting. Scumhunting is how the town wins the game, and pretending to scumhunt as mafia is how you make yourself look like a townie, so it’s how you make yourself harder for the town to find.

How exactly do you scumhunt? It’s often hard to tell. Plenty of experienced players struggle in knowing what to look for. There are some common tells that people use, like being suspicious of people who try to blend in, or being suspicious of people who overreact, or not being suspicious of people trying hard to scumhunt.

Unfortunately it’s never that simple. Most scumhunting is just intuition. Any of the above things can be benign in some contexts, good looks in some, and bad looks in others. It’s all on a case by case basis. Why do you think this person wrote this post? If the explanations for a townie writing a post are better than the explanations for scum writing the same post, it might earn a townread. If vice versa, maybe a scumread. If you can’t tell, maybe the one post just doesn’t matter. There are a lot of those posts that just don’t matter.

If you have an idea about whether someone is town or scum, don’t be shy, just say it. I and the other experienced players may appear to shoot your ideas down, but don’t be discouraged when that happens. We (me, Fvr_Vain, RTG) are here to help you learn the game, and trial and error is how you get there.

The word “scumhunting” might make you think you’re only looking for scum, but looking for town is also helpful; if you find a townie, that’s one less person you can accuse falsely. Finding town helps find scum this way. The same applies in reverse; if you find scum, you can name people that they aren’t likely to be partnered with, hence finding more townies. If you do both at once, you do both better.

Before we go further, do not claim your role for no reason. If you are a power role (any role with some special ability), you want to stay hidden so that the mafia doesn't just shoot you on the first night. If you aren't, you will narrow the possibilities of who can be one by claiming, which will make it harder for power roles to hide. For now, only claim if you are about to be hanged and the deadline is close.

Oh btw I’m town. Oh btw I’m scum.

Let the games begin.

The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player killed by the mafia. Night kills are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why [b]we need to hang as many times as possible.[/b]

Lastly, what will we actually do for most of this game? The bread and butter of Mafia is the day phases, where we just... talk. [b]The core idea is that you can often figure out if players are town or mafia just by reading their posts. This is also known as scumhunting.[/b] Scumhunting is how the town wins the game, and pretending to scumhunt as mafia is how you make yourself look like a townie, so it’s how you make yourself harder for the town to find.

How exactly do you scumhunt? It’s often hard to tell. Plenty of experienced players struggle in knowing what to look for. There are some common tells that people use, like being suspicious of people who try to blend in, or being suspicious of people who overreact, or not being suspicious of people trying hard to scumhunt.

Unfortunately it’s never that simple. [b]Most scumhunting is just intuition.[/b] Any of the above things can be benign in some contexts, good looks in some, and bad looks in others. It’s all on a case by case basis. Why do you think this person wrote this post? If the explanations for a townie writing a post are better than the explanations for scum writing the same post, it might earn a townread. If vice versa, maybe a scumread. If you can’t tell, maybe the one post just doesn’t matter. There are a lot of those posts that just don’t matter.

If you have an idea about whether someone is town or scum, don’t be shy, just say it. I and the other experienced players may appear to shoot your ideas down, but don’t be discouraged when that happens. [b]We (me, Fvr_Vain, RTG) are here to help you learn the game, and trial and error is how you get there.[/b]

The word “scumhunting” might make you think you’re only looking for scum, but looking for town is also helpful; if you find a townie, that’s one less person you can accuse falsely. Finding town helps find scum this way. The same applies in reverse; if you find scum, you can name people that they aren’t likely to be partnered with, hence finding more townies. If you do both at once, you do both better.

Before we go further, [b]do not claim your role for no reason.[/b] If you are a power role (any role with some special ability), you want to stay hidden so that the mafia doesn't just shoot you on the first night. If you aren't, you will narrow the possibilities of who can be one by claiming, which will make it harder for power roles to hide. For now, only claim if you are about to be hanged and the deadline is close.

Oh btw I’m town. Oh btw I’m scum.

Let the games begin.

post_rendered:
Pro tips

Don't give people townreads when you don't know on them. Leave them at null. The most important part of scumhunting tends to be accurate townreads, so that wolves can't just get away by not being scummy, and only focusing on whether or not someone has done something scummy, well, lets wolves get away by just not being scummy. I for one am gonna be towntelling my shoes off, and I wouldn't want that work to go into lumping me in with a bunch of nulls.

Don't make reads based on whether or not people are online. People have lives, it can be irritating. They could just have the tab open.

Don't scumread talking a lot on its own. It's just... not really scummy. In fact, in certain spheres, it's considered one of the towniest things you can do.

Anyway yeah you're probably both town.
