Usually the scum happen to be the people that are the least townie. There are many ways to usually tell if a new player like most of you are townie. Like aroodman said above being hyperactive tends to be a townier trait. However, it is only that way if it looks like you're attempting to make content to look for scum. I know looking back at my first ever scum game, I was hyperactive, but I was mostly fluffing and not really looking for scum because why would I? I already knew who all the scum were. That is why I really think friend is quite townie at this point because he's getting into the meat and potatoes of what mafia is about...trying to find scum by people's tendencies. Of course a lot of the scumtells are known by the experienced players so I can't use that same type of logic on aroodman. He knows what he's doing, so while he's being really helpful and providing reads, he would do the exact same thing as scum or town. So I don't want you guys to blindly trust aroodman, fvr, or me because statistically it is likely that one of us are mafia, so in future days when it is likely that 1 or more of us die, you can't blindly trust us otherwise that would make the game very easy for that experienced player to win the game.

Right now I'm gonna wait for the rest of the game to post before I make anymore reads, but right now I'd say friend is townie and the rest are null.

Yes I second aroodman with all statements made here.  I agree it is a lot easier to start by eliminating people as town than trying to find people as scum.  Usually the scum happen to be the people that are the least townie.  There are many ways to usually tell if a new player like most of you are townie.  Like aroodman said above being hyperactive tends to be a townier trait.  However, it is only that way if it looks like you're attempting to make content to look for scum.  I know looking back at my first ever scum game, I was hyperactive, but I was mostly fluffing and not really looking for scum because why would I?  I already knew who all the scum were.  That is why I really think friend is quite townie at this point because he's getting into the meat and potatoes of what mafia is about...trying to find scum by people's tendencies.  Of course a lot of the scumtells are known by the experienced players so I can't use that same type of logic on aroodman.  He knows what he's doing, so while he's being really helpful and providing reads, he would do the exact same thing as scum or town.  So I don't want you guys to blindly trust aroodman, fvr, or me because statistically it is likely that one of us are mafia, so in future days when it is likely that 1 or more of us die, you can't blindly trust us otherwise that would make the game very easy for that experienced player to win the game.

[/quote]

Don't scumread talking a lot on its own. It's just... not really scummy. In fact, in certain spheres, it's considered one of the towniest things you can do.

Anyway yeah you're probably both town.[/quote]

Yes I second aroodman with all statements made here.  I agree it is a lot easier to start by eliminating people as town than trying to find people as scum.  Usually the scum happen to be the people that are the least townie.  There are many ways to usually tell if a new player like most of you are townie.  Like aroodman said above being hyperactive tends to be a townier trait.  However, it is only that way if it looks like you're attempting to make content to look for scum.  I know looking back at my first ever scum game, I was hyperactive, but I was mostly fluffing and not really looking for scum because why would I?  I already knew who all the scum were.  That is why I really think friend is quite townie at this point because he's getting into the meat and potatoes of what mafia is about...trying to find scum by people's tendencies.  Of course a lot of the scumtells are known by the experienced players so I can't use that same type of logic on aroodman.  He knows what he's doing, so while he's being really helpful and providing reads, he would do the exact same thing as scum or town.  So I don't want you guys to blindly trust aroodman, fvr, or me because statistically it is likely that one of us are mafia, so in future days when it is likely that 1 or more of us die, you can't blindly trust us otherwise that would make the game very easy for that experienced player to win the game.

I don't like conftowning someone unless they are conf due to the fact that this could very well be wrong and hurt town down the line due do less analysis being done on those players and therefore less energy put into reading them.
[quote=aroodman]Al's absolutely never a wolf here
Way too pure with way too much conviction
If you want a spicy scumread that also has any chance of being right then friend, mj and Vain are all options, even if I wouldn't recommend going for any of them[/quote]
Exactly this.  Yes, they may very well be town, however immediately squashing any analysis of them in any way is not good in the grand scheme of things because they could have that 10% chance where they are actually scum and if we don't have analysis of that player to go back on at say LyLo, it gives us a lot less information to work with determining is someone is scum or town. or is there anything important youve noticed in the thread so far?[/quote]

[b]@aroodman[/b]
read
hot take
something whatever 3 2 1 go[quote name="Qinglang" url="/community/p22753375"]

[/quote][/quote]
where you're basically making an implied read off of someone else's posts.  So despite being present, unlike me, I don't really see anything of value in any of your posts besides the ones replying to me which I feel are the most useful posts you've had to this point.

systematic tryharding to avoid actually making hard decisions on reads is bad

ig qingling was scum

That is his last post.  He's done nothing but post one liners.  For an IC, this is not the sort of effort in posts I expect.  I expect in a newbie game for him to be trying to push town along to help make reads, help make activity (oops, now I'm the hypocrite), help the newbies understand the game.  Not sure I'd say it's scummy, but it is antitown.  You need to educate the players well enough with quality posts so that they know how to evaluate the game to find scum otherwise town just loses.  Scumlean
Al: Now I've talked about him above and I do agree with aroodman that he is townier rather than scummier, however I do disagree with the locktown mentality aroodman has employed. townlean
alien04: This guy has posted right? [quote=alien04]what do I do[/quote]
Oh yah he has, exactly once.  Well, I guess we'll wait for him to be replaced or starts posting to make a read.
friend: Similar boat to al without the aroodman locktown read.  I liked his play earlier on, however he has disappeared a bit more assuming due to the rest of town's inactivity. However, can you please explain this:
[quote=friend.]ah nevermind ignore me
[b]Unvote[/b][/quote]

candy: [quote=candythecorgi]rtg seems like town to me but idk i cant read experienced players especially when not everyone is an experienced player[/quote]
This isn't necessarily a scummy thing to say as a first timer as it can be overwhelming to go head to head with players who are more experienced.  Hell, I have played this game for 7 years and some players still intimidate me.  However, it is very important to voice your opinion.  If you do not voice your opinion we have less information about you and therefore have a harder time proving you to be town and scum, and guess what in a game like this the less presence you have, the more expendable you are.  So yes, please keep reading all the players.  Actually I'd like to see full detailed reads from everyone.  Besides some input on al, there really is not much here.  However I do find it interesting how he stuck up for al.  Could be some aroodman sheeping or possible buddying right here which is scummy in a way, but I'd rather hold judgment for when I get a full readlist from him.  I like looking for interactions like these because this is usually the easiest way to find scum and that's why everyone posting their reads are so useful to town analysis.  Scumlean
violet: Now I know aroodman has been all over qinglang from the get go and then he disappeared and I mentioned that that was scummy.  However, due to him being replaced I don't really consider it scummy anymore cause it's not like he came back after he disappeared.  However, violet has been nice and active since replacing in.  She confused her identity with qinglangs which I find a bit more townie.  WIFOM for sure but if he was scum he would have a lot more queues showing that he replaced qinglang like a mafia QT.  Also:

tl: rtg
null: everyone else

sr: aalaya
i might have misspelled something[/quote]

I like the effort so far however, I do notice that violet clears friend without really explaining why he believes that, and then proceeds to scumread al.  I find it funny how it's al and candy going up against friend and violet.  I'd like to see a lot more explanation rather than just saying town, null, or scum, but I really think that the contribution so far is townie.  Townlean

Anyway, I've now spent like 2 hours going through everything and creating this post.  Hopefully I don't fall this behind again but yah overall I'd like to see everyone analyze the game like this and provide reads about everyone.  Besides aroodman and violet, I haven't really seen anyone do much of this so far.

post_rendered:
When the first hollow steps are heard below, as the dwindling flames cast about their last warm glows, after the morning volume of the Granny Annals stands again in line upon the mantle, where the sunbeams just now skim its top… When the breaking of silence nears, Great Granny sinks for a moment into her deepest, most solemn, most sorrowful solitude. The soft light seems only to deepen the creases on her face; the faded tapestries swaying along the walls seem only to add to the stillness of her aspect.

Yet when the echoey clatter has nearly reached the door and I stir toward the door, glancing back briefly for reassurance, Great Granny seems hopeful again, smiling. Her eyes liven up in pleasant expectancy, and I grasp the latch resolutely and fling open the door with confidence.

"Good morning," she says gaily as each student offers her a little something from their knapsacks or baskets or lunchboxes ― in this manner they compensate her for teaching.
After they all seat themselves on their stools round the central table, the liveliest time of day, breakfast, begins. Before Great Granny withdraws a thick volume from the impeccable row of books, before she situates herself at the head of the table, before she ever opens her mouth and utters the words "duty" and "Unpaid Intern," she feasts with the apprentices ― today, on the bread, cheese, honey, and fresh fruits they've given her.

Seemingly all too soon, the clock chimes seven, but there rises no grumbling nor reluctance from the apprentices, only anticipation or the best shot at it from the more inattentive ones. It is at these sort of moments that I am most satisfied with the formidable work Great Granny has set on: transforming humans into Unpaid Interns worthy of carrying on the work of the Grannies. It is one thing to entrust humanity's fate to Great Granny and quite another to hand it to merely educated and trained humans, like me and this bunch of kids, with the label Unpaid Interns.

She stands now to select a volume from the shelf, preparing to commence the lesson, but then she suddenly staggers. And then she stumbles, backward, into the table, provoking a cry of dismayed surprise from the students. Yet through the din, Great Granny's steady voice, apparently unfazed, states simply, "Poison."
At this word, for a split second I am relieved, then with a realizing shudder, I start toward the door: "Doctor!" Before my foot even crosses the threshold and or even grazes the top step, however, the entire flight shivers and then shatters, piece by piece, with a resounding smash below, as with distress at Great Granny's fall. I stand still, stunned, but not for long ― duty calls. I rush over to Great Granny where she lies slumped against the table.

"Too late," she nearly croaks. "Two did this."
"Who?" I ask, but she doesn't seem to hear me.
After a pause she instead quotes, perhaps in a trance, the familiar passage: "Do your duty and a little more… it is the little more… that makes… the… difference."

In the utter silence, even her cracking murmur is distinctly heard.

Great Granny has passed away. She was The Great GrannyTM (Granny).
HannahW has been left in charge. She is an Unpaid InternTM (Moderator).

Day 1 has started on August 2, 2021 at 19:37 EDT and will end on August 16, 2021 at 19:37 EDT. Majority hang is off D1.

Usually the scum happen to be the people that are the least townie. There are many ways to usually tell if a new player like most of you are townie. Like aroodman said above being hyperactive tends to be a townier trait. However, it is only that way if it looks like you're attempting to make content to look for scum. I know looking back at my first ever scum game, I was hyperactive, but I was mostly fluffing and not really looking for scum because why would I? I already knew who all the scum were. That is why I really think friend is quite townie at this point because he's getting into the meat and potatoes of what mafia is about...trying to find scum by people's tendencies. Of course a lot of the scumtells are known by the experienced players so I can't use that same type of logic on aroodman. He knows what he's doing, so while he's being really helpful and providing reads, he would do the exact same thing as scum or town. So I don't want you guys to blindly trust aroodman, fvr, or me because statistically it is likely that one of us are mafia, so in future days when it is likely that 1 or more of us die, you can't blindly trust us otherwise that would make the game very easy for that experienced player to win the game.

Yes I second aroodman with all statements made here.  I agree it is a lot easier to start by eliminating people as town than trying to find people as scum.  Usually the scum happen to be the people that are the least townie.  There are many ways to usually tell if a new player like most of you are townie.  Like aroodman said above being hyperactive tends to be a townier trait.  However, it is only that way if it looks like you're attempting to make content to look for scum.  I know looking back at my first ever scum game, I was hyperactive, but I was mostly fluffing and not really looking for scum because why would I?  I already knew who all the scum were.  That is why I really think friend is quite townie at this point because he's getting into the meat and potatoes of what mafia is about...trying to find scum by people's tendencies.  Of course a lot of the scumtells are known by the experienced players so I can't use that same type of logic on aroodman.  He knows what he's doing, so while he's being really helpful and providing reads, he would do the exact same thing as scum or town.  So I don't want you guys to blindly trust aroodman, fvr, or me because statistically it is likely that one of us are mafia, so in future days when it is likely that 1 or more of us die, you can't blindly trust us otherwise that would make the game very easy for that experienced player to win the game.

[/quote]

She thinks she's right, and she cares about convincing others she's right. One or the other could be scum, but not both at once; scum want to either push wrong reads or just not push right reads, it's their win condition.

post_rendered:
Sometimes I know I have a responsibility to "teach the newbies" but I just... don't know where to begin

Anyway, for clarification on the not w/v read

mj's meltdown came specifically at the suggestion that he could be w/w with violet. He spent the whole conversation indirectly, preemptively assuming violet was a wolf, and until the thing mostly died down, he was too scared to outright admit that he thought I was tying him to violet, while still implying that concern throughout, and the whole reaction was incredibly raw.

The only logical conclusion is that mj was genuinely worried about what would happen to him in a w!violet world.

Could he feel this way if he's town and violet's a wolf? Yes, for obvious reasons.
Could he feel this way if he's town and violet is too? Yes. He's not in danger, but he doesn't know it.
Could he feel this way if he and violet are both wolves? Yes, he's worried I could dunk the whole team in that scenario.

Could he feel that way if he's a wolf and violet is town?

No, he knows there's no danger, he knows he can't get tied to w!violet because he knows w!violet doesn't exist.

post_rendered:
"Do your duty and a little more… It is the little more that makes the difference." ~ GA Henty


I would have spent the day phased out in a corner or sent the apprentices home somehow -- maybe a toss out the window -- but the last words of any departed person always superglues itself conspicuously in one's memory.

My duty? It would seem to be to avenge Great Granny. For whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed, I quote, mentally or aloud, I don't know, and from whence, I don't remember. It just popped into my mind. When Great Granny had expired this morning, the apprentices seemed to immediately though silently agree that duty meant vengeance. Even now they converse with agitation although there are lapses of apathy or despair.
I suppose Great Granny's dead body grown cold in the yonder corner…

But I turn away. If duty is vengeance, what possibly, would the little more be? Something like… torture? But it didn't make sense, not from Great Granny. Every single time I try to pin down her last words, try to even remotely understand their meaning, everything slips away… And I am left with the apprentices' resolution for revenge.
And that: it's justice is it not? -- the granting of what is proportioned to the worthiness of the recipient, the giving of what is deserved. But any excess or reserve, no matter the smallness, makes it injustice, so how can justice be carried a little further? It can only be reduced. Justice itself is only an infinitely small point of balance between two great weights…

The sun which rose so vividly this morning now sinks below the horizon, and its red glare floods the sky. In the tower we have our own miniature of the angry, red sun: fire and glowing ashes in the hearth. It will be dark soon, and we are all very serious.

"It's him," they tell me, pointing to violetmx12, and all around the dull, red faces nod.
"And?" I say, confused for heaven knows what reason. I just feel confused.
"violetmx12 did it," an apprentice puts in, "and that means death."
I nod but don't move, don't know what to say.

No one moves, and there is nothing left to say.

Then I realize maybe I am wrong about why I'm not moping alone in a corner. It's not just the last words of a deceased Great Granny, but the memory and teaching of a very alive one: month after month, year after year of a vision of the Unpaid Interns, the fulfillment of duty, that ever mysterious little more… and seeing the lurid face of the killer, that child there, makes me very angry. Revenge burns in my soul as well as the others'. It just took longer to surface through my meaninglessly thick web of thoughts. Duty demands vengeance.

I stand and start toward violetmx12, and the others, stirred up, follow me. Then we -- I -- impulsively press him/her toward the door and through it and down he falls where the stairwell once was, and I, nearly dizzy from the height, retreat rapidly and shut the door again.

The deed's done. Justice carried out.
I pace the length of the room once, twice, but am not satisfied. I am missing something, and I half know it. Then horror seizes me, and I stop. How am I to know if that kid was the murderer? Although I hadn't retreated to the corner, I had still zoned out in thought during their discussions… Although I hadn't tossed them all out the window, I had shoved one down to certain death on the stone floor below. And if he was innocent, what was I?

"Who was that?" I demand the apprentice closest to me.
"Uhh… violetmx12?" he hesitates.
"No I mean was he guilty?" I desperately ask.
"U-uhm," he hesitates again, sensing that I would not be satisfied with a personal conjecture.
Another apprentice walks up to us from the fire. "violetmx12 was a Fake Apprentice Intern (Mafia Goon)."


Night 1 has started 21:00 EST 8.16.21 and will end 21:00 EST 8.19.21.
